summary: Iter67+iter68 Crypto Staking module tested end-to-end. All 8 staking tests + 29 regression tests (messaging center + individual targeting) pass. Admin monitoring endpoint returns HTTP 200 with all required fields. Plan PUT/revert flow verified, settings hard-locked keys ignored as designed. Frontend StakingAdminTab renders correctly: stat cards, sync button, 3 plan rows, per-plan card, settings card with locked toggles all visible. Plan edit → save → toast success → table reflects new value verified via UI; revert to defaults confirmed (apy_bps=400, min=10, fee=1500). messaging_real_send_enabled remains 'false'.

design_issues.screen: Admin (any tab while wallet provider loads)
issues: WalletConnect (Web3Modal) console errors '401 invalid key' due to placeholder REACT_APP_WEB3MODAL_PROJECT_ID — not blocking but spams console; expected per agent context note

critical_code_review_comments: routes/staking.py admin_router uses prefix='/api/admin/staking' — make sure router is included exactly once in server.py to avoid duplicate route warnings.
admin_list_positions builds raw f-string SQL with limit value — clamp is fine (1..500) but consider parameterized query for consistency.
AdminPage.js is large (>1000 lines) — should eventually be split per-tab (StakingAdminTab is already extracted, follow that pattern for SettingsTab/ProAdminTab/etc).
Frontend uses axios withCredentials cookies; admin pytest uses Authorization Bearer — both work because backend get_current_user supports both. Good.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded plans + Bob's 500 MIR seeded by test fixture; plan_stake_3m reverted to defaults (apy_bps=400, min=10, fee=1500) post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use flag `-p no:pytest_ethereum` when running staking tests (web3 ships a conflicting pytest plugin). RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false recommended. Admin UI Staking tab is fully functional. Web3Modal placeholder errors in console are expected and non-blocking. messaging_real_send_enabled MUST stay 'false'.
rca of the issue: N/A — previous fixes (GROUP BY pl.sort_order in admin_monitoring + asyncpg statement_cache_size=0 for Neon pgbouncer) verified working: monitoring returns 200 reliably across multiple invocations including with admin_router and the frontend Promise.all triple-fetch.
